channel.title: Comments for page "Fire and Brimstone"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Fire and Brimstone"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some desired to cleanse the world in fire, a few with ice

Was that a Robert Frost reference, or am I overthinking it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Literally the only gripe I have is the little collapsible at the end. I feel like it doesn't add anything here. That said, this is easily the best take on a sympathetic devil I've ever seen. +1 for absolute excellence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did I miss this? Always happy to see new CE stuff. I might actually go and write another tale or two for this one. Glad to see it's not been abandoned.
Also +1, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really great addition to the Competitive Eschatology canon, and in all honesty it needs more attention. Hopefully the new Dooms Day contest going on will remind people to check back on this canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The return of my favourite canon! I was hovering over a no-vote until I saw that you added the italics and I’m glad to say that I feel confident that I can upvote the newest tale in this wonderful canon of death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Think it should be simplified a bit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to see that there are indeed other fans of the Canon.

I do like the italics idea, and re-reading it again I kinda have to agree. If y'all have any ideas let me know, but give me a day to think it over again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
COMPETITIVE ESCHATOLOGY YES

Ahem. That aside, the only real gripe I had with it was the formatting. I think it would be better if the monsters thoughts were in italics or something. Also his talking at the end is a little underwhelming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Prose is a little purple for my taste.
